newsSinceJun6: Stock fell -6.87% on June 9 (now -2.45% YTD) as progress toward a potential US-China rare earth agreement deflated the geopolitical scarcity premium across the rare-earth complex, compounded by insider-selling optics: CEO James Litinsky has sold ~1.29M shares (~$82M) over six months, including ~$26M April-June via 10b5-1 plans. No fundamental break: Q1 2026 showed record NdPr production (917t, +63% YoY) and sales (+117% YoY), revenue +119%, DoD and Apple contracts intact, Independence magnet sales still on track for H2 2026, and MP sued USA Rare Earth for alleged theft of its grain-boundary-diffusion magnet IP - itself a signal the magnet tech is strategically valuable.

sources.label: Simply Wall St - MP Materials (insider sales, drop drivers, Q1 metrics)
Rare Earth Exchanges - MP insider sales test the bull case
MINING.com - MP accuses USA Rare Earth of magnet tech theft
Motley Fool (June 8, 2026) - MP vs USA Rare Earth comparison

verdicts.rankRationale: On the physical-AI magnet-bottleneck lens, a -6.9% one-day flush driven by scarcity-premium unwind and insider-sale optics - not by any break in the magnets-for-actuators thesis - improves the asymmetry versus yesterday's hold at a higher price. Record NdPr output, DoD/Apple offtake, and H2 2026 Independence magnet sales keep the bottleneck case intact at a now-negative YTD entry.
verdicts.bull: The only scaled US mine-to-magnet chain, with record NdPr production (+63% YoY), DoD price-floor protection, Apple offtake, and first Independence magnet revenue in H2 2026 - a 7% flow-driven discount on an unchanged bottleneck thesis.
verdicts.neutral: A genuine US-China rare earth accord would structurally compress NdPr pricing and the strategic premium, partially offsetting the improved entry; the DoD floor insulates economics but not sentiment, so the reset may take time to digest.
verdicts.bear: CEO selling ~$82M of stock over six months into the strategic-premium narrative is a poor signal, and if Beijing normalizes rare earth exports, MP reverts to a high-cost producer whose magnet ramp is still pre-revenue.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: First commercial magnet sales from the Independence (Fort Worth) facility in H2 2026, converting the bottleneck narrative into reported revenue.
verdicts.primaryRisk: A formal US-China rare earth agreement that restores Chinese supply and collapses the Western scarcity premium on NdPr and magnets.
